text.title: Influencer Outreach, Compensation, and Campaign Execution
text.summary: Personalized outreach structure, compensation models, campaign brief requirements, and FTC compliance rules.

rule.statement: Reference a specific recent post by URL in every outreach message. Generic "I love your work on [TOPIC]" openers are flagged as mass outreach and ignored. Include a specific line from their content and explain why it resonated.
rule.rationale: Creators receive dozens of generic pitches daily. Proof of genuine engagement raises response rates 3–5x over templated outreach. Platform DM algorithms also rate-limit accounts sending identical messages.

rule.statement: Do not send outreach DMs autonomously via agent. Instagram and TikTok shadow-ban accounts exceeding ~50 DMs per day. Queue outreach for human confirmation before sending.
rule.rationale: Platform anti-spam systems cannot distinguish AI-bulk outreach from legitimate outreach. Account shadow-banning eliminates the brand's ability to reach any creator on that platform, not just the flagged campaign.

section.title: Compensation

rule.statement: For unproven creators, start with an affiliate + gifting hybrid. Convert top performers (with 60 days of data) to flat-fee retainers.
rule.rationale: Hybrid compensation aligns incentives — creators earn more when they drive results. Flat fees without performance data create risk: you are paying for reach that may not convert.

section.p: Compensation models: gifting (product awareness, free product), flat fee (specific deliverables), affiliate (commission per sale, performance-based), revenue share (% ongoing for long-term), hybrid (flat + affiliate).
Approximate rate benchmarks (Instagram post / YouTube video): Nano $50–250 / $200–500; Micro $250–1K / $500–2K; Mid $1K–5K / $2K–10K; Macro $5K–25K / $10K–50K.

rule.statement: Negotiate UGC reuse rights upfront: "90-day paid-social usage rights." Creator content reused in Meta/TikTok ads delivers 3–5x ROAS because it carries authentic social proof signals that native-brand ads lack.
rule.rationale: UGC rights are cheapest to acquire during the original negotiation. Post-campaign rights requests are often refused or priced at 2–5x the original content fee.

section.title: FTC Compliance

rule.statement: Every campaign brief must state: "All posts must include #ad or #sponsored in the first three hashtags or in visible text above the fold." Verify this in the post before and after go-live.
rule.rationale: FTC Endorsement Guides require clear and conspicuous disclosure. #ad buried in 30 hashtags or disclosed only in caption overflow does not meet the "conspicuous" standard. Non-compliance creates FTC investigation risk for both brand and creator.

section.reference: Campaign brief template including FTC disclosure requirement and approval process

rule.statement: Assign a unique discount code per creator (e.g., SARAH15). Codes survive iOS 14.5 tracking loss and cookie deprecation, provide clear per-creator attribution, and double as a customer incentive.
rule.rationale: UTM links break when users navigate between apps or share links. A creator-specific code is the most reliable cross-channel attribution signal available without third-party SDKs.
